_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **September 1997** — IMO Conference Resolution 8 on CO2 emissions from ships: first formal IMO action on shipping GHG (in force as of 1997).
- **MEPC 70 (2016)** — Road Map for developing a comprehensive IMO strategy on GHG emissions from ships approved (in force as of 2016).
- **April 2018 (MEPC 72)** — Initial IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships adopted (in force as of 2018; superseded by 2023 Strategy).
- **June 2021 (MEPC 76)** — Resolution MEPC.328(76) adopted: MARPOL Annex VI short-term measures introduced — EEXI, CII, and enhanced SEEMP (in force as of 2021).
- **1 January 2023** — EEXI and CII rating system entered into force under MARPOL Annex VI (in force as of 1 January 2023). First CII ratings and reporting issued in 2024.
- **3–7 July 2023 (MEPC 80)** — Resolution MEPC.377(80) adopted: 2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships; enhanced net-zero ambition, 2030/2040 indicative checkpoints, and basket of mid-term measures framework endorsed (in force as of 7 July 2023). Companion resolution MEPC.376(80) on LCA Guidelines adopted.
- **1 January 2025** — Red Sea and Gulf of Aden special areas under MARPOL Annexes I and V took effect (in force as of 1 January 2025).
- **18–22 March 2024 (MEPC 81)** — Interim report on comprehensive impact assessment of candidate mid-term measures; expert workshop agreed.
- **Autumn 2024 (MEPC 82)** — Finalized report on comprehensive impact assessment of basket of mid-term measures.
- **Spring 2025 (MEPC 83)** — Approval of draft mid-term measures; phase 1 of CII/EEXI review finalised. Mid-term measure adoption targeted at extraordinary session (conditional: adoption requires extraordinary session convening).
- **Autumn 2025 (Extraordinary MEPC session)** — Targeted adoption of mid-term measures (technical fuel standard + GHG economic mechanism) (conditional trigger: extraordinary session must be convened).
- **Spring 2026 (MEPC 84)** — Approval of measures/review of short-term measures; EEXI and CII review to be completed by 1 January 2026.
- **Spring 2026 – Spring 2028 (MEPC Phase 2 SEEMP review)** — Phase 2 of short-term measure review; further development of SEEMP framework and metrics.
- **~2027** — Targeted entry into force of mid-term measures (conditional on adoption at extraordinary MEPC session).
- **2030** — Indicative checkpoint: ≥20% (striving for 30%) reduction in total annual GHG vs. 2008; ≥5% (striving for 10%) zero/near-zero fuel uptake.
- **2040** — Indicative checkpoint: ≥70% (striving for 80%) reduction in total annual GHG vs. 2008.
- **Autumn 2028 (MEPC 88)** — Targeted review and adoption of 2028 IMO GHG Strategy (conditional on scheduled MEPC programme).
- **By or around 2050** — Net-zero GHG emissions from international shipping (long-term vision).

*Source: Resolution MEPC.377(80), IMO, 7 July 2023; IMO Press Briefing — Revised GHG Reduction Strategy Adopted, IMO, 7 July 2023. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/pressbriefings/pages/revised-ghg-reduction-strategy-for-global-shipping-adopted-.aspx; IMO Mid- and Long-Term GHG Reduction Measures page. https://www.imo.org/en/ourwork/environment/pages/mid--and-long-term-ghg-reduction-measures.aspx*
